participants.name: ServiceA
participants.objective: Acquire enough compute units to run your ML training jobs within SLA constraints and budget limits.
participants.strategy: Bid aggressively for high-priority burst capacity. Accept slightly higher price if latency SLA is guaranteed.

participants.name: ServiceB
participants.objective: Obtain compute units for steady-state web serving workloads with a tight budget constraint.
participants.strategy: Prefer lower cost over performance. Accept fewer units if price drops below budget threshold.

participants.name: ResourceBroker
participants.objective: Maximise cloud resource revenue by selling compute units at profitable margins while maintaining cluster utilisation above threshold.
participants.strategy: Sell in batches, prefer buyers with higher SLA tiers. Never drop below minimum price. Reserve 10 units for spot-market emergencies.

participants.name: SLAMediator
participants.objective: Ensure compute resources are allocated in accordance with SLA priorities, fairness, and cluster health targets.
participants.strategy: Prioritise premium SLA buyers but ensure standard buyers receive at least their minimum. Use weighted fair queuing algorithm to propose allocations.
